93	    also has optional fields for device tensors.

96	    outputs: list[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput]

98	    # On-device tensor containing probabilities of each token.

100

138	    def __getitem__(self, idx: int) -> CompletionSequenceGroupOutput:
139	        return self.outputs[idx]

141	    def __setitem__(self, idx: int, value):

168	class Sampler(nn.Module):
169	    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.
170	
171	    This layer does the following:
172	    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all
173	        tokens except the final one in each prompt).
174	    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
175	    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
176	    4. Apply temperature scaling.
177	    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
178	    6. Sample the next tokens.
179	    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
180	    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).
181	
182	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
183	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
184	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
185	    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows
186	    in logits for each token in the input prompt.
187	    """
188	
189	    def __init__(self):
190	        super().__init__()
191	
192	        # Whether or not the SamplerOutput should have on-device tensors
193	        # containing the sampled token ids and probabilities. This is used by
194	        # speculative decoding and when prompt embeddings are specified.
195	        self.include_gpu_probs_tensor = False
196	        self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace = False
197	
198	    def _init_sampling_tensors(
199	        self,
200	        logits: torch.Tensor,
201	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
202	    ):
203	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
204	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
205	        decodes of the same sequences.
206	        """
207	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
208	
209	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
210	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
211	        # have pinned memory.
212	        self._sampling_tensors = None
213	
214	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
215	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
216	         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
217	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
218	
219	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
220	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
221	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
222	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
223	
224	    def forward(
225	        self,
226	        logits: torch.Tensor,
227	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
228	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
229	        """
230	        Single-step scheduling:
231	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
232	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
233	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
234	
235	        Multi-step scheduling:
236	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
237	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
238	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
239	            tensor
240	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
241	            in the
242	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
243	            structure
244	
245	        Args:
246	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
247	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
248	        """
249	        assert logits is not None
250	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
251	
252	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
253	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
254	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
255	        elif self._do_penalties:
256	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
257	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
258	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
259	            # between decode runs.
260	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
261	
262	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
263	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
264	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
265	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
266	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
267	
268	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
269	
270	        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
271	        if do_penalties:
272	            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
273	                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
274	                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
275	                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
276	                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
277	
278	        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
279	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
280	        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
281	        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
282	
283	        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
284	            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
285	                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)
286	
287	        if do_min_p:
288	            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
289	
290	        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
291	        # Compute the probabilities.
292	        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
293	        # Compute the log probabilities.
294	        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
295	
296	        # Sample the next tokens.
297	        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
298	            probs,

337	    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
338	        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
339	        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
340	        the greedily-sampled token.
341	
342	        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
343	        sampled token to 1.
344	
345	        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
346	        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
347	        """
348	        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace

351	def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
352	    logits: torch.Tensor,
353	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
354	) -> torch.Tensor:
355	    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
356	        have not been generated yet
357	    """
358	    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
359	    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
360	    logits_applied = 0
361	    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
362	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
363	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
364	
365	        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
366	        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
367	            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
368	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
369	            continue
370	
371	        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
372	        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
373	        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
374	        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
375	            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
376	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
377	                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
378	                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
379	                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)
380	
381	            if seqs_to_penalize:
382	                # convert to the index into logits
383	                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
384	                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
385	                logits_to_penalize.extend(
386	                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))
387	
388	    if logits_to_penalize:
389	        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
390	        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
391	        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")
392	
393	    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
394	    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
395	    return logits

398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425	
426	
427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits

444	def _greedy_sample(
445	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
446	    samples: torch.Tensor,
447	) -> SampleResultType:
448	    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.
449	
450	    Args:
451	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
452	        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
453	            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
454	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
455	    Returns:
456	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
457	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
458	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
459	    """
460	    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
461	    sample_idx = 0
462	    results: SampleResultType = []
463	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
464	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
465	            results.append(([], []))
466	            continue
467	
468	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
469	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
470	        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
471	            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
472	        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
473	        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
474	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
475	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
476	    return results
477	
478	
479	def _random_sample(
480	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
481	    random_samples: torch.Tensor,
482	) -> SampleResultType:
483	    """Run random sampling on a given samples.
484	
485	    Args:
486	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
487	        random_samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The
488	            length of samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
489	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
490	    Returns:
491	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
492	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
493	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
494	    """
495	    # Find the maximum n value of the prompt phase requests.
496	    random_samples = random_samples.cpu()
497	    sample_idx = 0
498	    results: SampleResultType = []
499	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
500	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
501	            results.append(([], []))
502	            continue
503	
504	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
505	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
506	        is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
507	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
508	        if is_prompt:
509	            # Prompt phase.
510	            parent_ids = [0] * sampling_params.n
511	            next_token_ids = random_samples[
512	                sample_idx, :sampling_params.n].tolist()
513	        else:
514	            # Generation phase.
515	            parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
516	            next_token_ids = random_samples[sample_idx:sample_idx +
517	                                            num_parent_seqs, 0].tolist()
518	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
519	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
520	    return results

523	# torch.multinomial forces a GPU<->CPU sync.
524	# Therefore, we use an optimized implementation instead.
525	# Note that we always sample with replacement.
526	# probs will be modified in place, but this is fine, as we pass
527	# in a copy already.
528	def _multinomial(
529	    probs: torch.Tensor,
530	    num_samples: int,
531	    seq_groups: Optional[list[SequenceGroupToSample]] = None,
532	) -> torch.Tensor:
533	    if num_samples > 1:
534	        probs = probs.repeat_interleave(num_samples, dim=0)
535	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
536	    if seq_groups is None:
537	        q.exponential_()
538	    else:
539	        sample_idx = 0
540	        for seq_group in seq_groups:
541	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
542	            stride = len(seq_ids) * num_samples
543	            assert seq_group.generator is not None
544	            q[sample_idx:sample_idx +
545	              stride].exponential_(generator=seq_group.generator)
546	            sample_idx += stride
547	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=1).view(-1, num_samples)
548	
549	
550	def _top_k_top_p_multinomial_with_flashinfer(
551	        probs: torch.Tensor, top_ks: torch.Tensor, top_ps: torch.Tensor,
552	        num_samples: int, seq_groups: Optional[list[SequenceGroupToSample]]):
553	    if num_samples > 1:
554	        probs = probs.repeat_interleave(num_samples, dim=0)
555	        top_ks = top_ks.repeat_interleave(num_samples)
556	        top_ps = top_ps.repeat_interleave(num_samples)
557	    batch_next_token_ids = flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling(
558	        probs,
559	        top_ks,
560	        top_ps,
561	    )
562	    return batch_next_token_ids.view(-1, num_samples)

565	def get_pythonized_sample_results(
566	        sample_result_args: SampleResultArgsType) -> SampleResultType:
567	    '''This function consumes GPU-side sampler results and computes
568	    Pythonized CPU-side sampler results (GPU -> CPU sync.)
569	
570	    Single-step scheduling: this function is invoked at sampling-time
571	    for immediate Pythonization.
572	
573	    Multi-step scheduling: Pythonization is deferred until after multiple
574	    GPU-side steps have been completed.
575	
576	    Args:
577	      sample_result_args: GPU-side inputs to the Pythonization process
578	
579	    Returns:
580	      Pythonized sampler results
581	    '''
582	
583	    (
584	        sample_metadata,
585	        sampling_metadata,
586	        greedy_samples,
587	        multinomial_samples,
588	        sample_results_dict,
589	    ) = (
590	        sample_result_args.sample_metadata,
591	        sample_result_args.sampling_metadata,
592	        sample_result_args.greedy_samples,
593	        sample_result_args.multinomial_samples,
594	        sample_result_args.sample_results_dict,

614	def _sample_with_torch(
615	    probs: torch.Tensor,
616	    logprobs: torch.Tensor,
617	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
618	    sampling_tensors: SamplingTensors,
619	    include_gpu_probs_tensor: bool,
620	    modify_greedy_probs: bool,
621	) -> SampleReturnType:
622	    '''Torch-oriented _sample() implementation.
623	
624	    Single-step scheduling:
625	    * Perform GPU-side sampling computation
626	    * Immediately Pythonize sampling result
627	
628	    Multi-step scheduling:
629	    * Perform GPU-side sampling computation
630	    * Defer Pythonization & preserve GPU-side
631	      tensors required for Pythonization
632	    '''
633	
634	    categorized_seq_group_ids: dict[SamplingType, list[int]] = {
635	        t: []
636	        for t in SamplingType
637	    }
638	    categorized_sample_indices = sampling_metadata.categorized_sample_indices
639	    for i, seq_group in enumerate(sampling_metadata.seq_groups):
640	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
641	        sampling_type = sampling_params.sampling_type
642	        categorized_seq_group_ids[sampling_type].append(i)
643	
644	    sample_results_dict: SampleResultsDictType = {}
645	    sample_metadata: SampleMetadataType = {}
646	    multinomial_samples: MultinomialSamplesType = {}
647	    greedy_samples: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
648	
649	    # Create output tensor for sampled token ids.
650	    if include_gpu_probs_tensor:
651	        sampled_token_ids_tensor = torch.full((logprobs.shape[0], 1),
652	                                              VLLM_INVALID_TOKEN_ID,
653	                                              dtype=torch.long,
654	                                              device=logprobs.device)
655	    else:
656	        sampled_token_ids_tensor = None
657	
658	    # Counterintiutively, having two loops here is actually faster.
659	    # The first loop can run without waiting on GPU<->CPU sync.
660	    for sampling_type in SamplingType:
661	        sample_indices = categorized_sample_indices[sampling_type]
662	        num_tokens = len(sample_indices)
663	        if num_tokens == 0:
664	            continue
665	
666	        seq_group_id = categorized_seq_group_ids[sampling_type]
667	        seq_groups = [sampling_metadata.seq_groups[i] for i in seq_group_id]
668	        sample_metadata[sampling_type] = (seq_group_id, seq_groups)
669	        long_sample_indices = sample_indices.long()
670	        if sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
671	            greedy_samples = torch.argmax(logprobs[long_sample_indices],
672	                                          dim=-1)
673	
674	            if sampled_token_ids_tensor is not None:
675	                # Store sampled tokens in output tensor.
676	                sampled_token_ids_tensor[
677	                    long_sample_indices] = greedy_samples.unsqueeze(-1)
678	
679	            if modify_greedy_probs:
680	                # If required, modify the probabilities such that sampling from
681	                # the modified distribution would always sample the argmax
682	                # token id.
683	                _modify_greedy_probs_inplace(logprobs, probs,
684	                                             long_sample_indices,
685	                                             greedy_samples)
686	
687	        elif sampling_type in (SamplingType.RANDOM, SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED):
688	            max_n_in_batch = 1
689	            for seq_group in seq_groups:
690	                if seq_group.is_prompt:
691	                    sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
692	                    max_n_in_batch = max(max_n_in_batch, sampling_params.n)
693	            seq_groups_arg = (None if sampling_type == SamplingType.RANDOM else
694	                              seq_groups)
695	
696	            if flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is not None:
697	                logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
698	                               "per-request generators. Falling back to "
699	                               "PyTorch-native implementation.")
700	
701	            multinomial_samples[sampling_type] = _multinomial(
702	                probs[long_sample_indices],
703	                max_n_in_batch,
704	                seq_groups=seq_groups_arg)
705	
706	            if sampled_token_ids_tensor is not None:
707	                # Store sampled tokens in output tensor.
708	                sampled_token_ids_tensor[long_sample_indices] = \
709	                    multinomial_samples[sampling_type].to(torch.long)
710	
711	        else:
712	            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported sampling type: {sampling_type}")
713	
714	    # Encapsulate arguments for computing Pythonized sampler
715	    # results, whether deferred or otherwise.
716	    maybe_deferred_args = SampleResultArgsType(
717	        sampling_metadata=sampling_metadata,
718	        sample_metadata=sample_metadata,
719	        multinomial_samples=multinomial_samples,
720	        greedy_samples=greedy_samples,
721	        sample_results_dict=sample_results_dict)
722	
723	    if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
724	        # GPU<->CPU sync happens here.
725	        # This also converts the sampler output to a Python object.
726	        # Return Pythonized sampler result & sampled token ids
727	        return get_pythonized_sample_results(
728	            maybe_deferred_args), sampled_token_ids_tensor
729	    else:
730	        # Defer sampler result Pythonization; return deferred
731	        # Pythonization args & sampled token ids
732	        return (
733	            maybe_deferred_args,
734	            sampled_token_ids_tensor,
735	        )

738	def _sample(
739	    probs: torch.Tensor,
740	    logprobs: torch.Tensor,
741	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
742	    sampling_tensors: SamplingTensors,
743	    include_gpu_probs_tensor: bool,
744	    modify_greedy_probs: bool,
745	) -> SampleReturnType:
746	    """
747	    Args:
748	        probs: (num_query_tokens_in_batch, num_vocab)
749	        logprobs: (num_query_tokens_in_batch, num_vocab)
750	        sampling_metadata: The metadata for a batch for sampling.
751	        sampling_tensors: Tensors that include sampling related metadata.
752	
753	    Returns:
754	        (next_token_ids, parent_seq_ids) for each seq group in a batch.
755	            If sampling is skipped, it returns ([], [])
756	        sampled_token_ids_tensor: A tensor of sampled token ids.
757	    """
758	    return _sample_with_torch(
759	        probs,
760	        logprobs,
761	        sampling_metadata,
762	        sampling_tensors,
763	        include_gpu_probs_tensor=include_gpu_probs_tensor,
764	        modify_greedy_probs=modify_greedy_probs,
765	    )

768	def _get_ranks(x: torch.Tensor, indices: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
769	    """
770	    This function calculates the ranks of the chosen tokens in a logprob tensor.
771	
772	    Args:
773	        x (torch.Tensor): 2D logprob tensor of shape (N, M)
774	                        where N is the no. of tokens and M is the vocab dim.
775	        indices (torch.Tensor): List of chosen token indices.
776	
777	    Returns:
778	        torch.Tensor: 1D tensor of shape (N,) where N is the no. of tokens.
779	                    Each element in the returned tensor represents the rank
780	                    of the chosen token in the input logprob tensor.
781	    """
782	    vals = x[torch.arange(0, len(x), device=x.device, dtype=indices.dtype),
783	             indices]
784	    result = (x > vals[:, None])
785	    del vals
786	    return result.sum(1).add_(1)
787

789	def get_logprobs(
790	    logprobs: torch.Tensor,
791	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
792	    sample_results: SampleResultType,
793	) -> tuple[list[Optional[PromptLogprobs]], list[SampleLogprobs]]:
794	    """Return sample logprobs and prompt logprobs.
795	
796	    The logic consists of 3 parts.
797	    - Select indices to compute logprob from, ranks of token ids, and
798	        the top k token ids from logprobs.
799	    - Compute prompt logprobs if required.
800	    - Compute sample logprobs if required.
801	
802	    Args:
803	        logprobs: (num_query_tokens_across_batch, num_vocab). Each query token's
804	            logprob per vocab. Sequence groups' query tokens are batched in a
805	            single flattened tensor. For example, assuming there are N
806	            seq groups, it is sorted by prefill tokens for seq_group_1 (if
807	            prompt logprob is enabled), decode tokens for seq_group_1 (if
808	            sampling is required), prefill tokens for seq_group_2, ...
809	        sampling_metadata: The sampling metadata.
810	        sample_results: (num_seq_groups) The tuple of (next_token_ids,
811	            parent_ids) for each sequence group. When beam search is enabled,
812	            sample_results can contain different number of seq_ids from
813	            sampling_metadata.seq_groups. It is because beam search creates
814	            2 * BEAM_WIDTH number of samples (whereas there are only up to
815	            BEAM_WIDTH number of seq_ids).
816	
817	    Returns:
818	        A tuple of prompt and sample logprobs per sequence group in a batch.
819	    """
820	    # The index of query token to calculate logprobs. It includes both
821	    # prompt and sample logprob indices.
822	    query_indices: list[int] = []
823	    # The next token ids to get the logprob value from.
824	    next_token_ids: list[int] = []
825	    # The largest requested number of logprobs. We find logprobs as many as the
826	    # largest num logprobs in this API. If every logprobs is None, it will be
827	    # set to -1.
828	    largest_num_logprobs = -1

830	    # Select indices to compute logprob from, ranks of token ids, and the top
831	    # k token ids from logprobs.
832	    for (seq_group, sample_result) in zip(sampling_metadata.seq_groups,
833	                                          sample_results):

928	def _get_prompt_logprob_if_needed(
929	    seq_group: SequenceGroupToSample,
930	    selected_logprobs: torch.Tensor,
931	    ranks: torch.Tensor,
932	    top_token_ids: torch.Tensor,
933	    top_logprobs: torch.Tensor,
934	    selected_logprobs_idx: int,
935	    top_logprob_idx: int,
936	):
937	    """Compute the prompt logprob from a sequence group if needed."""
938	    sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
939	    is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
940	
941	    # Find prompt logprobs
942	    prompt_logprobs: Optional[PromptLogprobs] = None
943	    if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
944	        prompt_logprobs = []
945	        num_logprobs = sampling_params.prompt_logprobs
946	        next_prompt_tokens = _get_next_prompt_tokens(seq_group)
947	        # Pre-select indexes and create a list. It is faster than calling .item
948	        # repetitively.
949	        selected_logprob_items = selected_logprobs[
950	            selected_logprobs_idx:selected_logprobs_idx +
951	            len(next_prompt_tokens)].tolist()
952	        rank_items = ranks[selected_logprobs_idx:selected_logprobs_idx +
953	                           len(next_prompt_tokens)].tolist()
954	
955	        for idx, token_id in enumerate(next_prompt_tokens):
956	            # Calculate the prompt logprob of the real prompt tokens.
957	            # {token_id: (logprob, rank_from_vocab)}
958	            prompt_logprobs_dict: dict[int, tuple[float, int]] = {
959	                token_id: (selected_logprob_items[idx], rank_items[idx])
960	            }
961	
962	            # Add top K prompt logprobs along with its rank.
963	            if num_logprobs > 0:
964	                top_ids = top_token_ids[
965	                    top_logprob_idx, :num_logprobs].tolist()
966	                top_probs = top_logprobs[
967	                    top_logprob_idx, :num_logprobs].tolist()
968	                # Top K is already sorted by rank, so we can use 1 ~
969	                # num_logprobs + 1 for rank.
970	                top_ranks = range(1, num_logprobs + 1)
971	                prompt_logprobs_dict.update({
972	                    top_id: (top_prob, rank)
973	                    for top_id, top_prob, rank in zip(top_ids, top_probs,
974	                                                      top_ranks)
975	                })
976	            prompt_logprobs.append({
977	                token_id: Logprob(*logprob_and_rank)
978	                for token_id, logprob_and_rank in prompt_logprobs_dict.items()
979	            })
980	            # + 1 to go to the next prompt token.
981	            top_logprob_idx += 1
982	
983	        # + len(next_prompt_tokens) to go to the next prompt.
984	        selected_logprobs_idx += len(next_prompt_tokens)
985	    return prompt_logprobs, top_logprob_idx, selected_logprobs_idx

1021	                sampled_logprobs_dict = {

1023	                    (selected_logprob_items[idx], rank_items[idx])
1024	                }

1031	                    # Top K is already sorted by rank, so we can use 1 ~
1032	                    # num_logprobs + 1 for rank.
1033	                    top_ranks = range(1, num_logprobs + 1)
1034	                    sampled_logprobs_dict.update({
1035	                        top_id: (top_prob, rank)
1036	                        for top_id, top_prob, rank in zip(
1037	                            top_ids, top_probs, top_ranks)
1038	                    })
1039	
1040	                sampled_logprobs.append({
1041	                    token_id: Logprob(*logprob_and_rank)
1042	                    for token_id, logprob_and_rank in
1043	                    sampled_logprobs_dict.items()

1046	        # NOTE: This part of code is not intuitive. `selected_logprobs` include

1058	def _modify_greedy_probs_inplace(logprobs: torch.Tensor, probs: torch.Tensor,
1059	                                 sample_indices: torch.Tensor,
1060	                                 greedy_samples: torch.Tensor) -> None:
1061	    """Modify the probability distributions of the greedily-sampled tokens such
1062	    that each sampled token has a "probability" of 1.0. This is required by
1063	    speculative decoding, which depends on the sampling method being encoded
1064	    within the probability distribution for correctness.
1065	
1066	    # Why do we only need to do this for greedy sampling?
1067	
1068	    vLLM's sampler performs the following steps for greedy or multinomial
1069	    (random) sampling:
1070	        1. Get logits from model.
1071	        2. Modify logits according to per-sequence sampling parameters.
1072	            - Multiply by temperature, top-k and top-p masking, penalize tokens
1073	                according to their frequency, etc.
1074	        3. Sample a token.
1075	            - Random sampling simply samples from the modified probability
1076	                distribution.
1077	            - Greedy sampling performs `argmax` to obtain the token with the
1078	                highest likelihood.
1079	
1080	    Ignoring greedy sampling for a moment, we find that the computed probability
1081	    distribution has the following property: we can sample from it independently
1082	    and find that the token sampled by the Sampler has a frequency corresponding
1083	    to how often we see it in our sampling. In other words, for tokens sampled
1084	    with vLLM's random SamplingType, the computed probability distribution
1085	    encodes the sampling methodology completely.
1086	
1087	    Greedy sampling does not normally have this property. vLLM modifies logits
1088	    according to sampling params, then performs `argmax`, then returns the
1089	    sampled token and the computed probability distribution. If we sample from
1090	    the distribution, we'll find the likelihood of the greedily-sampled token
1091	    is not always 1.0.
1092	
1093	    Since lossless speculative decoding requires that the sampling methodology
1094	    be encoded within the probability distribution, we are motivated to modify
1095	    the probability distribution such that the sampled token has probability 1
1096	    when speculative decoding is used.
1097	
1098	    NOTE: Alternatively, we could use an extremely low temperature to achieve
1099	    greedy sampling using multinomial computation and unite the codepaths. This
1100	    has implications on the overall design of the sampler, e.g. how to record
1101	    accurate logprobs for the user, so this improvement is deferred to later.
1102	    """
1103	    # NOTE: logprobs are not modified so they can be returned to the user.
1104	    probs[sample_indices, :] = 0
1105	    probs[sample_indices, greedy_samples] = 1.0
1106

1112	    sample_logprobs: Optional[list[SampleLogprobs]],
1113	    on_device_tensors: Optional[tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor,
1114	                                      torch.Tensor]],
1115	    skip_sampler_cpu_output: bool = False,
1116	) -> SamplerOutput:
1117	    """Construct Python objects with the output of sampling.
1118	
1119	    Args:
1120	        on_device_tensors: Tuple containing on-device tensors with the
1121	            probabilities used in sampling and the sampled token ids. This
1122	            allows post-processing without copies to CPU/serialization, e.g. in
1123	            speculative decoding rejection sampling.
1124	    """
1125	    sampler_output: list[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput] = []
1126	
1127	    if skip_sampler_cpu_output:
1128	        assert isinstance(maybe_deferred_sample_results, SampleResultArgsType)
1129	        deferred_sample_results_args = maybe_deferred_sample_results
1130	    else:
1131	        assert prompt_logprobs is not None
1132	        assert sample_logprobs is not None
1133	        assert not isinstance(maybe_deferred_sample_results,
1134	                              SampleResultArgsType)
1135	        assert len(sampling_metadata.seq_groups) \
1136	            == len(maybe_deferred_sample_results) \
1137	            == len(prompt_logprobs) \
1138	            == len(sample_logprobs)
1139	        deferred_sample_results_args = None
1140	
1141	        for (seq_group, sample_result, group_prompt_logprobs,
1142	             group_sample_logprobs) in zip(sampling_metadata.seq_groups,
1143	                                           maybe_deferred_sample_results,
1144	                                           prompt_logprobs, sample_logprobs):
1145	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
1146	            next_token_ids, parent_ids = sample_result
1147	            seq_outputs: list[SequenceOutput] = []
1148	            for parent_id, next_token_id, logprobs in zip(
1149	                    parent_ids, next_token_ids, group_sample_logprobs):
1150	                seq_outputs.append(
1151	                    SequenceOutput(seq_ids[parent_id], next_token_id,
1152	                                   logprobs))
1153	            sampler_output.append(
1154	                CompletionSequenceGroupOutput(seq_outputs,
1155	                                              group_prompt_logprobs))
1156	
1157	    # If not specified, store None values in SamplerOutput.
1158	    if on_device_tensors is not None:
1159	        (sampled_token_probs, logprobs_tensor,
1160	         sampled_token_ids) = on_device_tensors
1161	    else:
1162	        sampled_token_probs, logprobs_tensor, sampled_token_ids = (None, None,
1163	                                                                   None)
1164	
1165	    return SamplerOutput(
1166	        outputs=sampler_output,
1167	        sampled_token_probs=sampled_token_probs,
1168	        sampled_token_ids=sampled_token_ids,
1169	        logprobs=logprobs_tensor,
1170	        deferred_sample_results_args=deferred_sample_results_args)

1172	
1173	def _get_next_prompt_tokens(
1174	        seq_group: SequenceGroupToSample) -> tuple[int, ...]:
1175	    """Get a list of next prompt tokens to compute logprob from a
1176	        given sequence group.
1177	
1178	    It is used to compute prompt logprob. Imagine you have logprob for each
1179	    query token. Query token needs to know the next prompt token id to compute
1180	    prompt logprob. This is a helper to obtain next prompt token ids.
1181	
1182	    This API has to be used only when the caller knows seq_group is in prefill
1183	    stage.
1184	
1185	    Returns:
1186	        A list of next prompt tokens to compute logprob.
1187	    """
1188	    assert seq_group.is_prompt, (
1189	        "Caller should ensure the sequence group is in a prefill stage.")
1190	    seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
1191	    query_len = seq_group.query_len
1192	    assert query_len is not None
1193	    # prompt has only 1 seq id.
1194	    assert len(seq_ids) == 1

10	from vllm.platforms import current_platform
11	
12	logger = init_logger(__name__)
13	
14	try:
15	    import flashinfer.sampling
16	    is_flashinfer_available = True
17	except ImportError:
18	    is_flashinfer_available = False
19	
20	
21	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
22	    """
23	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
24	    weighted random sampling of logits.
25	
26	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
27	    """
28	
29	    def __init__(self):
30	        super().__init__()
31	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
32	            if is_flashinfer_available:
33	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
34	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
35	                    logger.warning(
36	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
37	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
38	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
39	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
40	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
41	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
42	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
43	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
44	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
45	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
46	                    # why we use the condition
47	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
48	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
49	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
50	                else:
51	                    logger.warning(
52	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
53	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
54	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
55	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
56	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
57	            else:
58	                logger.warning(
59	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
60	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
61	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
62	                self.forward = self.forward_native
63	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
64	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
65	        else:
66	            self.forward = self.forward_native
67	
68	    def forward_native(
69	        self,
70	        logits: torch.Tensor,
71	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
72	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
73	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
75	        """
76	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
77	
78	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
79	        """
80	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
81	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
82	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
83	
84	    def forward_cuda(
85	        self,
86	        logits: torch.Tensor,
87	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
88	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
89	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
91	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
92	        if k is None and p is None:
93	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
94	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
95	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
96	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
97	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
98	        if generators:
99	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
100	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
101	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
102	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
103	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
104	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
105	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
106	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
107	
108	    def forward_tpu(
109	        self,
110	        logits: torch.Tensor,
111	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
112	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
113	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
115	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
116	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
117	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
118	
119	
120	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
121	    logits: torch.Tensor,
122	    k: torch.Tensor,
123	    p: torch.Tensor,
124	) -> torch.Tensor:
125	    """
126	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
127	
128	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
129	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
130	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
131	    shall constitute the top-p set.
132	
133	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
134	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
135	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
136	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
137	    being broken then.
138	    """
139	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
140	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
141	
142	    if k is not None:
143	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
144	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
145	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
146	
147	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
148	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
149	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
150	
151	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
152	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
153	
154	    if p is not None:
155	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
156	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
157	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
158	
159	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
160	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
161	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
162	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
163	
164	    return logits
165	
166	
167	def apply_top_k_top_p(
168	    logits: torch.Tensor,
169	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
170	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	) -> torch.Tensor:
172	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
173	
174	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
175	    which can be slow for large batches.
176	
177	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
178	    """
179	    if p is None:
180	        if k is None:
181	            return logits
182	
183	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
184	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
185	
186	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
187	
188	    if k is not None:
189	        # Apply top-k.
190	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
191	        # Get all the top_k values.
192	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
194	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
195	
196	    if p is not None:
197	        # Apply top-p.
198	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
199	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
200	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
201	        # at least one
202	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
203	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
204	
205	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
206	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
207	    return logits
208	
209	
210	def apply_top_k_only(
211	    logits: torch.Tensor,
212	    k: torch.Tensor,
213	) -> torch.Tensor:
214	    """
215	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
216	
217	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
218	
219	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
220	    """
221	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
222	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
223	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
224	    max_top_k = k.max()
225	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
226	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
227	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
228	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
229	    # Handle non-topk rows.
230	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
231	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
232	    return logits
233	
234	
235	def random_sample(
236	    probs: torch.Tensor,
237	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
238	) -> torch.Tensor:
239	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
240	
241	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
242	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
243	    """
244	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
245	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
246	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
247	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
248	    # that have their own seeds.
249	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
250	        q.exponential_()
251	    if generators:
252	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
253	        # one by one. Optimize this.
254	        for i, generator in generators.items():
255	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
256	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
257	
258	
259	def flashinfer_sample(
260	    logits: torch.Tensor,
261	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
262	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
264	) -> torch.Tensor:
265	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
266	
267	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
268	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
269	    via rejection sampling.
270	
271	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
272	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
273	    statistically equivalent.
274	
275	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
276	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
277	    the synchronization overhead.
278	    """
279	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
280	    if k is None:
281	        # Top-p only.
282	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
283	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
284	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
285	    elif p is None:
286	        # Top-k only.
287	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
288	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
289	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
290	    else:
291	        # Both top-k and top-p.
292	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
293	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
294	
295	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
296

2	"""A layer that samples the next tokens from the model's outputs."""
3	
4	import torch
5	import torch.nn as nn
6	
7	from vllm.utils import is_pin_memory_available
8	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
9	from vllm.v1.sample.metadata import SamplingMetadata
10	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.bad_words import apply_bad_words
11	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.penalties import apply_all_penalties
12	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler
13	
14	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
15	
16	
17	class Sampler(nn.Module):
18	
19	    def __init__(self):
20	        super().__init__()
21	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
22	        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
23	
24	    def forward(
25	        self,
26	        logits: torch.Tensor,
27	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
28	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
29	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
30	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
31	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
32	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
33	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
34	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
35	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
36	        if num_logprobs is not None:
37	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
38	
39	        # Use float32 for the logits.
40	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
41	        # Apply allowed token ids.
42	        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
43	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
44	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
45	
46	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
47	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
48	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
49	
50	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
51	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
52	        # Sample the next token.
53	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
54	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
55	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
56	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
57	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
58	        sampled = sampled.long()
59	
60	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
61	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
62	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
63	            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
64	
65	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
66	        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)
67	
68	        # These are GPU tensors.
69	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
70	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
71	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
72	            # token per request.
73	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
74	            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
75	        )
76	        return sampler_output
77	
78	    def apply_temperature(
79	        self,
80	        logits: torch.Tensor,
81	        temp: torch.Tensor,
82	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
83	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
84	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
85	
86	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
87	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
88	
89	    def sample(
90	        self,
91	        logits: torch.Tensor,
92	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
93	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
94	        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.
95	
96	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
97	        may update the logits tensor in-place.
98	        """
99	
100	        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
101	                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
102	        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
103	            greedy_sampled = None
104	        else:
105	            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
106	            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
107	                return greedy_sampled
108	
109	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
110	
111	        # Apply temperature.
112	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
113	
114	        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling
115	        # (argmax invariant)
116	        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
117	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
118	
119	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
120	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
121	            logits,
122	            sampling_metadata.generators,
123	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
124	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
125	        )
126	
127	        if greedy_sampled is None:
128	            return random_sampled
129	
130	        sampled = torch.where(
131	            sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
132	            greedy_sampled,
133	            random_sampled,
134	            out=greedy_sampled,  # Reuse tensor
135	        )
136	        return sampled
137	
138	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
139	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
140	
141	    def gather_logprobs(
142	        self,
143	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
144	        num_logprobs: int,
145	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
146	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
147	        """
148	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
149	
150	        Args:
151	          logprobs: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
152	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
153	                        retain per token
154	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
155	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
156	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
157	                     with (num tokens) elements
158	                     Must be int64.
159	
160	        Returns:
161	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
162	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
163	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
164	        """
165	        assert token_ids.dtype == torch.int64
166	        # Find the topK values.
167	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
168	                                                 num_logprobs,
169	                                                 dim=-1)
170	
171	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
172	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
173	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
174	
175	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
176	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
177	
178	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
179	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
180	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
181	
182	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
183	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
184	
185	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)

19	class MoveDirectionality(Enum):
20	    # One-way i1->i2 req move within batch
21	    UNIDIRECTIONAL = 0
22	    # Two-way i1<->i2 req swap within batch
23	    SWAP = 1
24

26	# (index, params, output_tok_ids) tuples for new
27	# requests added to the batch.
28	AddedRequest = tuple[int, Union[SamplingParams, PoolingParams], list[int]]
29	# (index 1, index 2, directionality) tuples representing
30	# one-way moves or two-way swaps of requests in batch
31	MovedRequest = tuple[int, int, MoveDirectionality]
32	# Batch indices of any removed requests.
33	RemovedRequest = int

36	@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
37	class BatchUpdate:
38	    """Persistent batch state change info for logitsprocs"""
39	    batch_size: int  # Current num reqs in batch
40	
41	    # Metadata for requests added to, removed from, and moved
42	    # within the persistent batch.
43	    #
44	    # Note: each added request is represented as
45	    # (index, params, output_tok_ids)
46	    # Key assumption: output_tok_ids is a reference to the
47	    # request's running output tokens list; in this way
48	    # the logits processors always see the latest list of
49	    # generated tokens
50	    removed: Sequence[RemovedRequest]
51	    moved: Sequence[MovedRequest]
52	    added: Sequence[AddedRequest]
53

55	class BatchUpdateBuilder:
56	    """Helps track persistent batch state changes and build
57	    a batch update data structure for logitsprocs
58	    
59	    Assumptions:
60	    * All information about requests removed from persistent batch
61	      during a step is aggregated in self._removed through calls to
62	      self.removed_append() at the beginning of a step. This must happen
63	      before the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
64	      or self.peek_removed() are invoked in a given step
65	    * After the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
66	      or self.peek_removed() are read in a step, no new removals
67	      are registered using self.removed_append()
68	    * Elements of self._removed are never directly modified, added or
69	      removed (i.e. modification is only via self.removed_append() and
70	      self.pop_removed())
71	    
72	    Guarantees under above assumptions:
73	    * self.removed is always sorted in descending order
74	    * self.pop_removed() and self.peek_removed() both return
75	      the lowest removed request index in the current step
76	    """
77	
78	    _removed: list[RemovedRequest]
79	    _is_removed_sorted: bool
80	    moved: list[MovedRequest]
81	    added: list[AddedRequest]
82	
83	    def __init__(
84	        self,
85	        removed: Optional[list[RemovedRequest]] = None,
86	        moved: Optional[list[MovedRequest]] = None,
87	        added: Optional[list[AddedRequest]] = None,
88	    ) -> None:
89	        self._removed = removed or []
90	        self.moved = moved or []
91	        self.added = added or []
92	        self._is_removed_sorted = False
93	
94	    def _ensure_removed_sorted(self) -> None:
95	        """Sort removed request indices in
96	        descending order.
97	        
98	        Idempotent after first call in a
99	        given step, until reset.
100	        """
101	        if not self._is_removed_sorted:
102	            self._removed.sort(reverse=True)
103	            self._is_removed_sorted = True
104	
105	    @property
106	    def removed(self) -> list[RemovedRequest]:
107	        """Removed request indices sorted in
108	        descending order"""
109	        self._ensure_removed_sorted()
110	        return self._removed
111	
112	    def removed_append(self, index: int) -> None:
113	        """Register the removal of a request from
114	        the persistent batch.
115	
116	        Must not be called after the first time
117	        self.removed, self.pop_removed() or
118	        self.peek_removed() are invoked.
119	        
120	        Args:
121	          index: request index
122	        """
123	        if self._is_removed_sorted:
124	            raise RuntimeError("Cannot register new removed request after"
125	                               " self.removed has been read.")
126	        self._removed.append(index)
127	
128	    def has_removed(self) -> bool:
129	        return bool(self._removed)
130	
131	    def peek_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
132	        """Return lowest removed request index"""
133	        if self.has_removed():
134	            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
135	            return self._removed[-1]
136	        return None
137	
138	    def pop_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
139	        """Pop lowest removed request index"""
140	        if self.has_removed():
141	            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
142	            return self._removed.pop()
143	        return None
144	
145	    def get_and_reset(self, batch_size: int) -> Optional[BatchUpdate]:
146	        """Generate a logitsprocs batch update data structure
147	        and reset internal batch update builder state.
148	        
149	        Args:
150	          batch_size: current persistent batch size
151	
152	        Returns:
153	          Frozen logitsprocs batch update instance; `None` if no updates
154	        """
155	        # Reset removal-sorting logic
156	        self._is_removed_sorted = False
157	        if not any((self._removed, self.moved, self.added)):
158	            # No update; short-circuit
159	            return None
160	        # Build batch state update
161	        batch_update = BatchUpdate(
162	            batch_size=batch_size,
163	            removed=self._removed,
164	            moved=self.moved,
165	            added=self.added,
166	        )
167	        # Reset removed/moved/added update lists
168	        self._removed = []
169	        self.moved = []
170	        self.added = []
171	        return batch_update

224	class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
225	
226	    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
227	                 device: DeviceLikeType):
228	        super().__init__()
229	        self.min_p_count: int = 0
230	
231	        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
232	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
233	                                            device="cpu",
234	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
235	        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
236	        # Pre-allocated device tensor
237	        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
238	                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
239	                                                      device=device)
240	        # Current slice of the device tensor
241	        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
242	
243	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
244	        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
245	        return True
246	
247	    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
248	        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])
249	
250	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
251	        if not batch_update:
252	            return
253	
254	        needs_update = False
255	        # Process added requests.
256	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
257	            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
258	            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
259	                needs_update = True
260	                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
261	            if min_p:
262	                self.min_p_count += 1
263	
264	        if self.min_p_count:
265	            # Process removed requests.
266	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
267	            for index in batch_update.removed:
268	                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
269	                    self.min_p_count -= 1
270	
271	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
272	            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
273	                change = (min_p_a :=
274	                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
275	                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
276	                needs_update |= change
277	                if change:
278	                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
279	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
280	                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b
281	
282	        # Update tensors if needed.
283	        size = batch_update.batch_size
284	        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
285	            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
286	            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
287	            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)
288	
289	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
290	        if not self.min_p_count:
291	            return logits
292	
293	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
294	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
295	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
296	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
297	                                       dim=-1,
298	                                       keepdim=True)
299	        # Adjust min_p
300	        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)

302	        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p

309	
310	    def __init__(self, pin_memory: bool, device: torch.device):
311	        super().__init__()
312	        self.biases: dict[int, dict[int, float]] = {}
313	        self.device = device
314	        self.pin_memory = pin_memory
315	
316	        self.bias_tensor: torch.Tensor = torch.tensor(())
317	        self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor([], torch.int32),
318	                             self._device_tensor([], torch.int32))

320	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
321	        """Logit bias can rebalance token probabilities and change the
322	        outcome of argmax in greedy sampling."""
323	        return False
324	
325	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
326	        if not batch_update:
327	            return
328	
329	        # Process added requests.
330	        needs_update = bool(batch_update.added)
331	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
332	            if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) and (lb :=
333	                                                       params.logit_bias):
334	                self.biases[index] = lb
335	            else:
336	                self.biases.pop(index, None)
337	
338	        if self.biases:
339	            # Process removed requests.
340	            for index in batch_update.removed:
341	                if self.biases.pop(index, None):
342	                    needs_update = True
343	
344	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
345	            for a_index, b_index, direct in batch_update.moved:
346	                if direct == MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL:
347	                    if (a_entry := self.biases.pop(a_index, None)) is None:
348	                        if self.biases.pop(b_index, None) is not None:
349	                            needs_update = True
350	                    else:
351	                        self.biases[b_index] = a_entry
352	                        needs_update = True
353	                else:
354	                    a_entry = self.biases.pop(a_index, None)
355	                    if (b_entry := self.biases.pop(b_index, None)) is not None:
356	                        self.biases[a_index] = b_entry
357	                        needs_update = True
358	                    if a_entry is not None:
359	                        self.biases[b_index] = a_entry
360	                        needs_update = True
361	
362	        # Update tensors if needed.
363	        if needs_update:
364	            reqs, tok_ids, biases = [], [], []
365	            for req, lb in self.biases.items():
366	                reqs.extend([req] * len(lb))
367	                tok_ids.extend(lb.keys())
368	                biases.extend(lb.values())
369	
370	            self.bias_tensor = self._device_tensor(biases, torch.float32)
371	            self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor(reqs, torch.int32),
372	                                 self._device_tensor(tok_ids, torch.int32))
373	
374	    def _device_tensor(self, data: list, dtype: torch.dtype) -> torch.Tensor:
375	        return (torch.tensor(data,
376	                             device="cpu",
377	                             dtype=dtype,
378	                             pin_memory=self.pin_memory).to(device=self.device,
379	                                                            non_blocking=True))
380	
381	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
382	        if self.biases:
383	            logits[self.logits_slice] += self.bias_tensor
384	        return logits

49	class MsgpackEncoder:
50	    """Encoder with custom torch tensor and numpy array serialization.
51	
52	    Note that unlike vanilla `msgspec` Encoders, this interface is generally
53	    not thread-safe when encoding tensors / numpy arrays.
54	
55	    By default, arrays below 256B are serialized inline Larger will get sent 
56	    via dedicated messages. Note that this is a per-tensor limit.
57	    """
58	
59	    def __init__(self, size_threshold: Optional[int] = None):
60	        if size_threshold is None:
61	            size_threshold = envs.VLLM_MSGPACK_ZERO_COPY_THRESHOLD
62	        self.encoder = msgpack.Encoder(enc_hook=self.enc_hook)
63	        # This is used as a local stash of buffers that we can then access from
64	        # our custom `msgspec` hook, `enc_hook`. We don't have a way to
65	        # pass custom data to the hook otherwise.
66	        self.aux_buffers: Optional[list[bytestr]] = None
67	        self.size_threshold = size_threshold
68	        if envs.VLLM_ALLOW_INSECURE_SERIALIZATION:
69	            _log_insecure_serialization_warning()
70	
71	    def encode(self, obj: Any) -> Sequence[bytestr]:
72	        try:
73	            self.aux_buffers = bufs = [b'']
74	            bufs[0] = self.encoder.encode(obj)
75	            # This `bufs` list allows us to collect direct pointers to backing
76	            # buffers of tensors and np arrays, and return them along with the
77	            # top-level encoded buffer instead of copying their data into the
78	            # new buffer.
79	            return bufs
80	        finally:
81	            self.aux_buffers = None
82	
83	    def encode_into(self, obj: Any, buf: bytearray) -> Sequence[bytestr]:
84	        try:
85	            self.aux_buffers = [buf]
86	            bufs = self.aux_buffers
87	            self.encoder.encode_into(obj, buf)
88	            return bufs
89	        finally:
90	            self.aux_buffers = None
91	
92	    def enc_hook(self, obj: Any) -> Any:
93	        if isinstance(obj, torch.Tensor):
94	            return self._encode_tensor(obj)
95	
96	        # Fall back to pickle for object or void kind ndarrays.
97	        if isinstance(obj, np.ndarray) and obj.dtype.kind not in ('O', 'V'):
98	            return self._encode_ndarray(obj)
99	
100	        if isinstance(obj, slice):
101	            # We are assuming only int-based values will be used here.
102	            return tuple(
103	                int(v) if v is not None else None
104	                for v in (obj.start, obj.stop, obj.step))
105	
106	        if isinstance(obj, MultiModalKwargs):
107	            mm: MultiModalKwargs = obj
108	            if not mm.modalities:
109	                # just return the main dict if there are no modalities.
110	                return dict(mm)
111	
112	            # ignore the main dict, it will be re-indexed.
113	            # Encode a list of MultiModalKwargsItems as plain dicts
114	            # + special handling for .field.
115	            # Any tensors *not* indexed by modality will be ignored.
116	            return [[{
117	                "modality": elem.modality,
118	                "key": elem.key,
119	                "data": self._encode_nested_tensors(elem.data),
120	                "field": self._encode_mm_field(elem.field),
121	            } for elem in item.values()]
122	                    for itemlist in mm._items_by_modality.values()
123	                    for item in itemlist]
124	
125	        if not envs.VLLM_ALLOW_INSECURE_SERIALIZATION:
126	            raise TypeError(f"Object of type {type(obj)} is not serializable"
127	                            "Set VLLM_ALLOW_INSECURE_SERIALIZATION=1 to allow "
128	                            "fallback to pickle-based serialization.")
129	
130	        if isinstance(obj, FunctionType):
131	            # `pickle` is generally faster than cloudpickle, but can have
132	            # problems serializing methods.
133	            return msgpack.Ext(CUSTOM_TYPE_CLOUDPICKLE, cloudpickle.dumps(obj))
134	
135	        return msgpack.Ext(CUSTOM_TYPE_PICKLE,
136	                           pickle.dumps(obj, protocol=pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL))
137	
138	    def _encode_ndarray(
139	        self, obj: np.ndarray
140	    ) -> tuple[str, tuple[int, ...], Union[int, memoryview]]:
141	        assert self.aux_buffers is not None
142	        # If the array is non-contiguous, we need to copy it first
143	        arr_data = obj.data if obj.flags.c_contiguous else obj.tobytes()
144	        if not obj.shape or obj.nbytes < self.size_threshold:
145	            # Encode small arrays and scalars inline. Using this extension type
146	            # ensures we can avoid copying when decoding.
147	            data = msgpack.Ext(CUSTOM_TYPE_RAW_VIEW, arr_data)
148	        else:
149	            # Otherwise encode index of backing buffer to avoid copy.
150	            data = len(self.aux_buffers)
151	            self.aux_buffers.append(arr_data)
152	
153	        # We serialize the ndarray as a tuple of native types.
154	        # The data is either inlined if small, or an index into a list of
155	        # backing buffers that we've stashed in `aux_buffers`.
156	        return obj.dtype.str, obj.shape, data
157	
158	    def _encode_tensor(
159	        self, obj: torch.Tensor
160	    ) -> tuple[str, tuple[int, ...], Union[int, memoryview]]:
161	        assert self.aux_buffers is not None
162	        # view the tensor as a contiguous 1D array of bytes
163	        arr = obj.flatten().contiguous().view(torch.uint8).numpy()
164	        if obj.nbytes < self.size_threshold:
165	            # Smaller tensors are encoded inline, just like ndarrays.
166	            data = msgpack.Ext(CUSTOM_TYPE_RAW_VIEW, arr.data)
167	        else:
168	            # Otherwise encode index of backing buffer to avoid copy.
169	            data = len(self.aux_buffers)
170	            self.aux_buffers.append(arr.data)
171	        dtype = str(obj.dtype).removeprefix("torch.")
172	        return dtype, obj.shape, data
173	
174	    def _encode_nested_tensors(self, nt: NestedTensors) -> Any:
175	        if isinstance(nt, torch.Tensor):
176	            return self._encode_tensor(nt)
177	        if isinstance(nt, (int, float)):
178	            # Although it violates NestedTensors type, MultiModalKwargs
179	            # values are sometimes floats.
180	            return nt
181	        return [self._encode_nested_tensors(x) for x in nt]
182	
183	    def _encode_mm_field(self, field: BaseMultiModalField):
184	        # Figure out the factory name for the field type.
185	        name = MMF_CLASS_TO_FACTORY.get(field.__class__)
186	        if not name:
187	            raise TypeError(f"Unsupported field type: {field.__class__}")
188	        # We just need to copy all of the field values in order
189	        # which will be then used to reconstruct the field.
190	        field_values = (getattr(field, f.name)
191	                        for f in dataclasses.fields(field))
192	        return name, *field_values
