195	class MsgpackDecoder:
196	    """Decoder with custom torch tensor and numpy array serialization.
197	
198	    Note that unlike vanilla `msgspec` Decoders, this interface is generally
199	    not thread-safe when encoding tensors / numpy arrays.
200	    """
201	
202	    def __init__(self, t: Optional[Any] = None):
203	        args = () if t is None else (t, )
204	        self.decoder = msgpack.Decoder(*args,
205	                                       ext_hook=self.ext_hook,
206	                                       dec_hook=self.dec_hook)
207	        self.aux_buffers: Sequence[bytestr] = ()
208	        if envs.VLLM_ALLOW_INSECURE_SERIALIZATION:
209	            _log_insecure_serialization_warning()
210	
211	    def decode(self, bufs: Union[bytestr, Sequence[bytestr]]) -> Any:
212	        if isinstance(bufs, (bytes, bytearray, memoryview, zmq.Frame)):
213	            # TODO - This check can become `isinstance(bufs, bytestr)`
214	            # as of Python 3.10.
215	            return self.decoder.decode(bufs)
216	
217	        self.aux_buffers = bufs
218	        try:
219	            return self.decoder.decode(bufs[0])
220	        finally:
221	            self.aux_buffers = ()
222	
223	    def dec_hook(self, t: type, obj: Any) -> Any:
224	        # Given native types in `obj`, convert to type `t`.
225	        if isclass(t):
226	            if issubclass(t, np.ndarray):
227	                return self._decode_ndarray(obj)
228	            if issubclass(t, torch.Tensor):
229	                return self._decode_tensor(obj)
230	            if t is slice:
231	                return slice(*obj)
232	            if issubclass(t, MultiModalKwargs):
233	                if isinstance(obj, list):
234	                    return MultiModalKwargs.from_items(
235	                        self._decode_mm_items(obj))
236	                return MultiModalKwargs({
237	                    k: self._decode_nested_tensors(v)
238	                    for k, v in obj.items()
239	                })
240	        return obj
241	
242	    def _decode_ndarray(self, arr: Any) -> np.ndarray:
243	        dtype, shape, data = arr
244	        # zero-copy decode. We assume the ndarray will not be kept around,
245	        # as it now locks the whole received message buffer in memory.
246	        buffer = self.aux_buffers[data] if isinstance(data, int) else data
247	        return np.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=dtype).reshape(shape)
248	
249	    def _decode_tensor(self, arr: Any) -> torch.Tensor:
250	        dtype, shape, data = arr
251	        # Copy from inline representation, to decouple the memory storage
252	        # of the message from the original buffer. And also make Torch
253	        # not complain about a readonly memoryview.
254	        buffer = self.aux_buffers[data] if isinstance(data, int) \
255	            else bytearray(data)
256	        torch_dtype = getattr(torch, dtype)
257	        assert isinstance(torch_dtype, torch.dtype)
258	        if not buffer:  # torch.frombuffer doesn't like empty buffers
259	            assert 0 in shape
260	            return torch.empty(shape, dtype=torch_dtype)
261	        # Create uint8 array
262	        arr = torch.frombuffer(buffer, dtype=torch.uint8)
263	        # Convert back to proper shape & type
264	        return arr.view(torch_dtype).view(shape)
265	
266	    def _decode_mm_items(self, obj: list) -> list[MultiModalKwargsItem]:
267	        decoded_items = []
268	        for item in obj:
269	            elems = []
270	            for v in item:
271	                v["data"] = self._decode_nested_tensors(v["data"])
272	                # Reconstruct the field processor using MultiModalFieldConfig
273	                factory_meth_name, *field_args = v["field"]
274	                factory_meth = getattr(MultiModalFieldConfig,
275	                                       factory_meth_name)
276	
277	                # Special case: decode the union "slices" field of
278	                # MultiModalFlatField
279	                if factory_meth_name == "flat":
280	                    field_args[0] = self._decode_nested_slices(field_args[0])
281	
282	                v["field"] = factory_meth(None, *field_args).field
283	                elems.append(MultiModalFieldElem(**v))
284	            decoded_items.append(MultiModalKwargsItem.from_elems(elems))
285	        return decoded_items
286	
287	    def _decode_nested_tensors(self, obj: Any) -> NestedTensors:
288	        if isinstance(obj, (int, float)):
289	            # Although it violates NestedTensors type, MultiModalKwargs
290	            # values are sometimes floats.
291	            return obj
292	        if not isinstance(obj, list):
293	            raise TypeError(f"Unexpected NestedTensors contents: {type(obj)}")
294	        if obj and isinstance(obj[0], str):
295	            return self._decode_tensor(obj)
296	        return [self._decode_nested_tensors(x) for x in obj]
297	
298	    def _decode_nested_slices(self, obj: Any) -> Any:
299	        assert isinstance(obj, (list, tuple))
300	        if obj and not isinstance(obj[0], (list, tuple)):
301	            return slice(*obj)
302	        return [self._decode_nested_slices(x) for x in obj]
303	
304	    def ext_hook(self, code: int, data: memoryview) -> Any:
305	        if code == CUSTOM_TYPE_RAW_VIEW:
306	            return data
307	
308	        if envs.VLLM_ALLOW_INSECURE_SERIALIZATION:
309	            if code == CUSTOM_TYPE_PICKLE:
310	                return pickle.loads(data)
311	            if code == CUSTOM_TYPE_CLOUDPICKLE:
312	                return cloudpickle.loads(data)
313	
314	        raise NotImplementedError(
315	            f"Extension type code {code} is not supported")
316

97	        # Prevent directly appending to these lists since
98	        # they should also be updated simultaneously.
99	        self.output_token_ids = ConstantList(self._output_token_ids)
100	        self.all_token_ids = ConstantList(self._all_token_ids)
101	
102	        # State
103	        # The number of tokens with prefix cache hits.
104	        self.num_cached_tokens = -1
105	
106	        # The number of NaNs in logits. A value greater than 0
107	        # indicates that the output is corrupted
108	        self.num_nans_in_logits = 0
109	
110	    @classmethod
111	    def from_engine_core_request(cls, request: EngineCoreRequest) -> "Request":
112	        if request.mm_inputs is not None:
113	            assert isinstance(request.mm_inputs, list)
114	            assert is_list_of(request.mm_inputs, MultiModalKwargs), (
115	                "mm_inputs was not updated in EngineCore.add_request")
116	
117	        return cls(
118	            request_id=request.request_id,
119	            client_index=request.client_index,
120	            prompt_token_ids=request.prompt_token_ids,
121	            multi_modal_inputs=request.mm_inputs,
122	            multi_modal_hashes=request.mm_hashes,
123	            multi_modal_placeholders=request.mm_placeholders,
124	            sampling_params=request.sampling_params,
125	            pooling_params=request.pooling_params,
126	            eos_token_id=request.eos_token_id,
127	            arrival_time=request.arrival_time,
128	            lora_request=request.lora_request,
129	            structured_output_request=StructuredOutputRequest(
130	                sampling_params=request.sampling_params) \
131	                    if request.sampling_params else None,
132	            cache_salt=request.cache_salt,
133	            priority=request.priority,
134	        )
135	
136	    def append_output_token_ids(
137	        self,
138	        token_ids: Union[int, list[int]],
139	    ) -> None:
140	        if isinstance(token_ids, int):
141	            self._output_token_ids.append(token_ids)
142	            self._all_token_ids.append(token_ids)
143	        else:
144	            self._output_token_ids.extend(token_ids)
145	            self._all_token_ids.extend(token_ids)
146	
147	    @property
148	    def is_output_corrupted(self) -> bool:
149	        return self.num_nans_in_logits > 0
150	
151	    @property
152	    def num_tokens(self) -> int:
153	        return len(self._all_token_ids)
154	
155	    @property
156	    def num_tokens_with_spec(self) -> int:
157	        return len(self._all_token_ids) + len(self.spec_token_ids)
158	
159	    @property
160	    def num_output_tokens(self) -> int:
161	        return len(self._output_token_ids)
162	
163	    def is_finished(self) -> bool:
164	        return RequestStatus.is_finished(self.status)
165	
166	    def get_finished_reason(self) -> Union[FinishReason, None]:
167	        return RequestStatus.get_finished_reason(self.status)
168	
169	    def get_num_encoder_tokens(self, input_id: int) -> int:
170	        assert input_id < len(self.mm_positions)
171	        num_tokens = self.mm_positions[input_id].length
172	        return num_tokens
173	
174	    @property
175	    def use_structured_output(self) -> bool:
176	        return self.sampling_params is not None and \
177	            self.sampling_params.guided_decoding is not None
178	
179	    def record_event(
180	        self,
181	        event_type: EngineCoreEventType,
182	        timestamp: Optional[float] = None,
183	    ) -> None:
184	        self.events.append(EngineCoreEvent.new_event(event_type, timestamp))
185	
186	    def take_events(self) -> Optional[list[EngineCoreEvent]]:
187	        if not self.events:
188	            return None
189	        events, self.events = self.events, []
190	        return events
191	
192	
193	class RequestStatus(enum.IntEnum):
194	    """Status of a request."""
195	    WAITING = enum.auto()
196	    WAITING_FOR_FSM = enum.auto()
197	    WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS = enum.auto()
198	    RUNNING = enum.auto()
199	    PREEMPTED = enum.auto()
200	    # Note: anything after PREEMPTED will be considered
201	    # as a finished status.
202	    FINISHED_STOPPED = enum.auto()
203	    FINISHED_LENGTH_CAPPED = enum.auto()
204	    FINISHED_ABORTED = enum.auto()
205	    FINISHED_IGNORED = enum.auto()
206	
207	    def __str__(self):
208	        return self.name
209	
210	    @staticmethod
211	    def is_finished(status: "RequestStatus") -> bool:
212	        return status > RequestStatus.PREEMPTED
213	
214	    @staticmethod
215	    def get_finished_reason(
216	            status: "RequestStatus") -> Union[FinishReason, None]:
217	        return _FINISHED_REASON_MAP.get(status)
218	
219	
220	# Mapping of finished statuses to their finish reasons.
221	# NOTE: The ignored requests are the requests whose prompt lengths
222	# are longer than the model's length cap. Therefore, the stop
223	# reason should also be "length" as in OpenAI API.
224	_FINISHED_REASON_MAP = {
225	    RequestStatus.FINISHED_STOPPED: FinishReason.STOP,
226	    RequestStatus.FINISHED_LENGTH_CAPPED: FinishReason.LENGTH,
227	    RequestStatus.FINISHED_ABORTED: FinishReason.ABORT,
228	    RequestStatus.FINISHED_IGNORED: FinishReason.LENGTH,
229	}

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed report of my findings.

## Comprehensive Analysis of vLLM Sampling Logic

Based on my exploration of the vLLM codebase, here's a complete understanding of how sampling logic (top_p, top_k, min_p filtering) is implemented:

---

### 1. **Main Sampler Files**

There are two main sampling implementations in vLLM:

**V0 (Original) Sampler:**
- `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (1204 lines) - Main sampler class
- `/csrc/sampler.cu` - CUDA kernels for penalty application

**V1 (New) Sampler:**
- `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` - Simplified V1 sampler
- `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` - Modular top-k/top-p filtering

---

### 2. **How Sampling Parameters Flow Through the System**

**Entry Point - SamplingParams** (`/vllm/sampling_params.py` lines 118-250):
```
Key fields:
- temperature: float = 1.0
- top_p: float = 1.0           # Nucleus sampling threshold
- top_k: int = 0                # Number of top tokens to keep
- min_p: float = 0.0           # Minimum probability relative to max
- presence_penalty: float = 0.0
- frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
- repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
```

**Metadata Layer** (`/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`):
- `SequenceGroupToSample` dataclass (lines 20-62): Carries `sampling_params` for each sequence group
- `SamplingTensors` dataclass (lines 373-598): Batches sampling parameters into tensors
  - Extracts `top_ps`, `top_ks`, `min_ps` into separate tensors for GPU processing
  - Also batches `temperatures`, `presence_penalties`, `frequency_penalties`, `repetition_penalties`

**Tensor Preparation** (`/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` lines 387-495):
```python
def from_sampling_metadata():
    # Lines 415-420: Extract individual parameters
    top_p = sampling_params.top_p
    min_p = sampling_params.min_p
    top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)  # Cap top_k at vocab size
    
    # Lines 429-430: Detect if min_p is used
    if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
        do_min_p = True
```

---

### 3. **Top-P (Nucleus Sampling) Implementation**

**Location:** Multiple implementations depending on platform/backend

**V0 Implementation** (`/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` lines 398-424):
```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, p, k):
    # Step 1: Sort logits in descending order
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # Step 2: Apply top-k filtering (set to -inf)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Step 3: Apply top-p filtering
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)  # Cumulative sum
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)  # Keep only top p%
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # Ensure at least one token
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Step 4: Re-sort back to original order
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(
        dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**V1 Implementation** (`/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` lines 167-207):
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p):
    # Similar approach but with more nuance
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    if k is not None:
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    if p is not None:
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**TPU Optimized Version** (`/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` lines 120-164):
- Avoids slow `torch.scatter` operations on TPU
- Uses "cut-off" element approach instead

**FlashInfer Backend** (`/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` lines 259-295):
- Optionally uses `flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits` for faster execution
- Requires FlashInfer >= 0.2.3

---

### 4. **Top-K Implementation**

**Eager Mode** (`/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` lines 210-232):
```python
def apply_top_k_only(logits, k):
    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]  # No-op if k == vocab_size
    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
    max_top_k = k.max()
    
    # Use torch.topk for efficiency
    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**Key Detail:** Top-k filtering only happens if top-p is not specified or if using combined top-k+top-p. When using only top-k, vLLM avoids the expensive full sort operation.

---

### 5. **Min-P Implementation**

**Location:** Both V0 and V1 implementations

**V0 Version** (`/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` lines 427-441):
```python
def _apply_min_p(logits, min_p):
    """
    min_p = threshold relative to max probability in vocab
    
    Formula: keep tokens where p(token) >= min_p * max_p(vocab)
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)  # Get max prob per sequence
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs  # Scale by max
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p  # Find low-prob tokens
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**V1 Version** (`/vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` lines 224-305):
```python
class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
    def apply(self, logits):
        # Convert logits to probability distribution
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        
        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
        
        # Adjust min_p: scaled_min_p = min_p * max_probability
        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
        
        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
        
        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits
```

**Detection:** Min-p is only applied if `min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS` (1e-5), line 429 in `sampling_metadata.py`

    └→ _sample() (lines 738-765)

```

---

### 7. **Processing Order in Sampler.forward()**

**V0 Sampler** (`/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` lines 268-294):
1. **Apply min_tokens penalty** - Line 268: `_apply_min_tokens_penalty()`
2. **Apply penalties** (presence, frequency, repetition) - Lines 271-276

4. **Top-k/Top-p filtering** - Lines 283-285
5. **Min-p filtering** - Lines 287-288

7. **Multinomial sampling** - Line 297-305

if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:

```

**Greedy Sampling** (`/vllm/sampling_params.py` lines 377-382):

- Top-p no-op if `p == 1.0`

**BatchNormalization in Tensors:**
- Each sequence group can have different parameters
- Parameters are batched in `SamplingTensors` with pinned memory for fast GPU transfer
- Conditional masking ensures parameters only apply to relevant tokens

---

### 9. **CUDA/Performance Optimizations**

**In-place Operations:**
- Line 281: `logits.div_()` for temperature scaling (avoids copy)

## Comprehensive Analysis of vLLM Sampling Logic

Based on my exploration of the vLLM codebase, here's a complete understanding of how sampling logic (top_p, top_k, min_p filtering) is implemented:

---

### 1. **Main Sampler Files**

There are two main sampling implementations in vLLM:

**V0 (Original) Sampler:**
- `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (1204 lines) - Main sampler class
- `/csrc/sampler.cu` - CUDA kernels for penalty application

**V1 (New) Sampler:**
- `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` - Simplified V1 sampler
- `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` - Modular top-k/top-p filtering

---

### 2. **How Sampling Parameters Flow Through the System**

**Entry Point - SamplingParams** (`/vllm/sampling_params.py` lines 118-250):
```
Key fields:
- temperature: float = 1.0
- top_p: float = 1.0           # Nucleus sampling threshold
- top_k: int = 0                # Number of top tokens to keep
- min_p: float = 0.0           # Minimum probability relative to max
- presence_penalty: float = 0.0
- frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
- repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
```

**Metadata Layer** (`/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`):
- `SequenceGroupToSample` dataclass (lines 20-62): Carries `sampling_params` for each sequence group
- `SamplingTensors` dataclass (lines 373-598): Batches sampling parameters into tensors
  - Extracts `top_ps`, `top_ks`, `min_ps` into separate tensors for GPU processing
  - Also batches `temperatures`, `presence_penalties`, `frequency_penalties`, `repetition_penalties`

**Tensor Preparation** (`/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` lines 387-495):
```python
def from_sampling_metadata():
    # Lines 415-420: Extract individual parameters
    top_p = sampling_params.top_p
    min_p = sampling_params.min_p
    top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)  # Cap top_k at vocab size
    
    # Lines 429-430: Detect if min_p is used
    if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
        do_min_p = True
```

---

### 3. **Top-P (Nucleus Sampling) Implementation**

**Location:** Multiple implementations depending on platform/backend

**V0 Implementation** (`/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` lines 398-424):
```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, p, k):
    # Step 1: Sort logits in descending order
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # Step 2: Apply top-k filtering (set to -inf)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Step 3: Apply top-p filtering
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)  # Cumulative sum
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)  # Keep only top p%
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # Ensure at least one token
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Step 4: Re-sort back to original order
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(
        dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**V1 Implementation** (`/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` lines 167-207):
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p):
    # Similar approach but with more nuance
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    if k is not None:
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    if p is not None:
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**TPU Optimized Version** (`/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` lines 120-164):
- Avoids slow `torch.scatter` operations on TPU
- Uses "cut-off" element approach instead

**FlashInfer Backend** (`/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` lines 259-295):
- Optionally uses `flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits` for faster execution
- Requires FlashInfer >= 0.2.3

---

### 4. **Top-K Implementation**

**Eager Mode** (`/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` lines 210-232):
```python
def apply_top_k_only(logits, k):
    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]  # No-op if k == vocab_size
    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
    max_top_k = k.max()
    
    # Use torch.topk for efficiency
    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**Key Detail:** Top-k filtering only happens if top-p is not specified or if using combined top-k+top-p. When using only top-k, vLLM avoids the expensive full sort operation.

---

### 5. **Min-P Implementation**

**Location:** Both V0 and V1 implementations

**V0 Version** (`/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` lines 427-441):
```python
def _apply_min_p(logits, min_p):
    """
    min_p = threshold relative to max probability in vocab
    
    Formula: keep tokens where p(token) >= min_p * max_p(vocab)
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)  # Get max prob per sequence
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs  # Scale by max
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p  # Find low-prob tokens
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**V1 Version** (`/vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` lines 224-305):
```python
class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
    def apply(self, logits):
        # Convert logits to probability distribution
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        
        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)

        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
        
        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p

        return logits
```

**Detection:** Min-p is only applied if `min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS` (1e-5), line 429 in `sampling_metadata.py`

---

### 6. **Data Structure Flow**

```
SamplingParams (sampling_params.py)
    ↓
SequenceGroupToSample (sampling_metadata.py:20-62)
    │ sampling_params field
    ↓
SamplingMetadata.prepare() (sampling_metadata.py:149-188)
    ↓
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata() (sampling_metadata.py:387-495)
    │ Creates tensors for:
    │ - temperatures
    │ - top_ps
    │ - top_ks
    │ - min_ps
    │ - presence_penalties
    │ - frequency_penalties
    │ - repetition_penalties
    ↓
Sampler.forward() (sampler.py:224-334)
    │ Conditional execution:
    │ - if do_top_p_top_k: _apply_top_k_top_p()
    │ - if do_min_p: _apply_min_p()
    │
    ├→ _apply_top_k_top_p() (lines 398-424)
    ├→ _apply_min_p() (lines 427-441)
    └→ _sample() (lines 738-765)
        └→ _sample_with_torch() (lines 614-735)
```

---

### 7. **Processing Order in Sampler.forward()**

**V0 Sampler** (`/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` lines 268-294):
1. **Apply min_tokens penalty** - Line 268: `_apply_min_tokens_penalty()`
2. **Apply penalties** (presence, frequency, repetition) - Lines 271-276
3. **Temperature scaling** - Lines 280-281
4. **Top-k/Top-p filtering** - Lines 283-285
5. **Min-p filtering** - Lines 287-288
6. **Softmax** - Lines 292-294: Compute probabilities and log-probabilities
7. **Multinomial sampling** - Line 297-305

**Detection Logic** (lines 426-434):
```python
if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS or top_k != vocab_size):
    do_top_p_top_k = True
if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
    do_min_p = True
```

---

### 8. **Special Cases and Optimizations**

**Greedy Sampling** (`/vllm/sampling_params.py` lines 377-382):
```python
if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:  # _SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
    self.top_p = 1.0      # Disable top-p
    self.top_k = 0        # Disable top-k
    self.min_p = 0.0      # Disable min-p
```

**No-op Detection:**
- Top-k no-op if `k == vocab_size` (lines 221, 148-149 in topk_topp_sampler.py)

**BatchNormalization in Tensors:**
- Each sequence group can have different parameters
- Parameters are batched in `SamplingTensors` with pinned memory for fast GPU transfer
- Conditional masking ensures parameters only apply to relevant tokens

---

### 9. **CUDA/Performance Optimizations**

**In-place Operations:**
- Line 281: `logits.div_()` for temperature scaling (avoids copy)
- Line 437: `min_p.unsqueeze_()` in min_p calculation

**FlashInfer Integration:**

- Falls back to PyTorch if not available or per-request generators used

**Tensor Pinning:**

---

### 10. **Key Files Summary**

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `vllm/sampling_params.py` | 603 | Parameter definitions & validation |
| `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 598 | Metadata & tensor preparation |

| `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` | 226 | V1 simplified sampler |

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile my findings into a comprehensive report.

## Summary Report: vLLM Sampling Parameters

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase and completed analysis of how sampling parameters are defined, validated, and used. Here are my detailed findings:

### 1. SamplingParams Class Definition

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331278241-tecb6b/vllm/sampling_params.py` (lines 118-589)

**Key Characteristics:**
- Implemented as a `msgspec.Struct` (lines 119-122) with `omit_defaults=True` and `dict=True` options, enabling efficient serialization/deserialization
- Uses `@dataclass`-like structure but with msgspec for better performance
- Includes comprehensive docstring (lines 123-203) referencing OpenAI text completion API

**Parameters Related to Your Query (top_p, top_k, min_p):**

```python
top_p: float = 1.0  # Line 212
# Docstring: "Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens 
# to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens."

top_k: int = 0  # Line 213
# Docstring: "Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
# to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens."

min_p: float = 0.0  # Line 214
# Docstring: "Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be 
# considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token. 
# Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this."
```

**Other Notable Sampling Parameters:**
- `n`: Number of output sequences (default: 1)
- `temperature`: Randomness control (default: 1.0) - critical for sampling strategy
- `presence_penalty`, `frequency_penalty`, `repetition_penalty`: Token penalty controls
- `seed`: For reproducible random generation
- `stop`, `stop_token_ids`: Generation termination
- `logprobs`, `prompt_logprobs`: Log probability tracking
- `bad_words`: Token exclusion list
- `guided_decoding`: Structured output control (with GuidedDecodingParams)
- `logit_bias`: Token-specific bias
- `allowed_token_ids`: Token allowlist

### 2. Validation Logic

**Validation Method:** `_verify_args()` (lines 387-462)

**Specific Validation for Key Parameters:**

**top_p validation (line 417-418):**
```python
if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
```
- Must be strictly greater than 0 and less than or equal to 1
- Allows inclusive endpoint at 1.0

**top_k validation (lines 420-425):**
```python
# quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
if self.top_k < -1:
    raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                     f"got {self.top_k}.")
if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
    raise TypeError(
        f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
```
- Must be integer type
- Can be -1 (disabled) or >= 1, or 0 (disabled)
- Rejects values < -1

**min_p validation (lines 426-428):**
```python
if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
                     f"{self.min_p}.")
```
- Must be in closed interval [0, 1] (inclusive on both ends)
- Allows 0.0 to disable

**Temperature-Based Logic (lines 377-382):**
When temperature < epsilon (1e-5), greedy sampling is triggered:
```python
if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
    # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
    self.top_p = 1.0
    self.top_k = 0
    self.min_p = 0.0
    self._verify_greedy_sampling()
```
- Automatically overrides top_p, top_k, and min_p for deterministic (greedy) sampling
- For greedy sampling, n must equal 1 (line 465-467)

**Other Critical Validations:**
- Temperature must be non-negative (line 414-416)
- presence_penalty, frequency_penalty must be in [-2.0, 2.0] (lines 404-409)
- repetition_penalty must be > 0 (lines 410-413)
- max_tokens, min_tokens relationships validated (lines 429-438)
- best_of >= n requirement (lines 335-338, 393-403)

### 3. Post-Initialization Processing

**`__post_init__()` Method (lines 327-385):**

The post-initialization handles:
1. **best_of/n normalization**: If best_of is set, it becomes n and original n becomes _real_n
2. **Temperature clamping**: Warns if temperature is too low (< 1e-2) and clamps it
3. **Seed normalization**: Converts -1 seed to None
4. **Stop handling**: Converts stop string to list, ensures non-empty strings
5. **Bad words handling**: Initializes empty list if None
6. **Logprobs conversion**: Converts boolean True to integer 1
7. **Stop token ID collection**: Updates _all_stop_token_ids set
8. **Output buffer length**: Calculates for stop string evaluation

### 4. Serialization and Deserialization

**Mechanism: msgspec.Struct with JSON Support**

The class uses msgspec's efficient binary serialization:
- `msgspec.Struct` provides automatic msgpack serialization
- Supports JSON encoding/decoding through msgspec.json
- `dict=True` parameter enables dictionary-like access
- Custom fields like `logits_processors` use `Any` type due to msgspec limitations (line 231)

**Key Serialization Patterns:**
- Fields are marked with `msgspec.field()` for special handling (e.g., default factories)
- Constraints encoded via `msgspec.Meta()` for validation hints:
  ```python
  truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
  ```

### 5. Conversion from API Requests to SamplingParams

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331278241-tecb6b/vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

**Factory Method: `SamplingParams.from_optional()` (lines 252-325)**

This static factory method:
- Normalizes None values to defaults
- Clamps logit_bias between -100 and 100 per OpenAI spec
- Converts token IDs in logit_bias to integers
- Returns a new SamplingParams instance

**Request Conversion Methods:**

**ChatCompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()** (lines 452-543):
- Applies request-level defaults
- Falls back to model/engine defaults
- Handles response_format for guided decoding
- Processes tool choices
- Sets output_kind based on streaming

**CompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()** (lines 897-981):
- Similar logic with different defaults
- Handles echo_without_generation case

**Default Parameters Template:**
```python
_DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS: dict = {
    "repetition_penalty": 1.0,
    "temperature": 1.0,
    "top_p": 1.0,
    "top_k": 0,
    "min_p": 0.0,
}
```

### 6. Helper Methods and Properties

**`update_from_generation_config()`** (lines 469-493):
- Updates stop_token_ids from model's generation config
- Adds EOS token IDs appropriately

**`update_from_tokenizer()`** (lines 495-529):
- Tokenizes bad_words into token IDs
- Handles prefix space variations
- Validates token IDs against vocabulary

**`sampling_type` property** (lines 531-537):
- Returns SamplingType enum (GREEDY, RANDOM, or RANDOM_SEED)
- Based on temperature and seed values

**`clone()` method** (lines 548-561):
- Deep copy with special handling for LogitsProcessor objects
- Handles parallel decoding scenarios

### 7. Related Classes

**GuidedDecodingParams** (lines 32-107):
- Separate dataclass for structured output constraints
- Mutually exclusive fields: json, regex, choice, grammar, json_object
- Supports multiple backends with validation

**BeamSearchParams** (lines 591-602):
- Separate parameters for beam search decoding
- Similar but distinct from standard sampling

**RequestOutputKind enum** (lines 109-116):
- Controls output format: CUMULATIVE, DELTA, FINAL_ONLY

### 8. Testing and Examples

**Test Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331278241-tecb6b/tests/test_sampling_params.py`: Unit tests for parameter validation
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331278241-tecb6b/tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py`: End-to-end integration tests

**Common Parameter Combinations Tested:**
- Basic penalties (temperature, presence_penalty, frequency_penalty, repetition_penalty)
- Probability controls (top_p, top_k, min_p)
- Stop conditions (stop strings, stop_token_ids)
- Output options (detokenize, skip_special_tokens, logprobs)
- Bad words filtering
- Seeded sampling for reproducibility

---

**Key Insights:**
1. **msgspec Usage**: The class leverages msgspec for efficient serialization, crucial for distributed inference scenarios
2. **Validation Strictness**: top_p requires open interval on lower bound (>0) but closed on upper (≤1); min_p uses closed interval [0,1]
3. **Temperature-Driven Logic**: Temperature < 1e-5 completely changes sampling behavior (forces greedy decoding)
4. **Default Fallback Chain**: API request → model config → engine defaults
5. **OpenAI Compatibility**: Closely follows OpenAI's text completion API specification while adding vLLM-specific extensions

## Summary Report: vLLM Sampling Parameters

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase and completed analysis of how sampling parameters are defined, validated, and used. Here are my detailed findings:

### 1. SamplingParams Class Definition

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331278241-tecb6b/vllm/sampling_params.py` (lines 118-589)

**Key Characteristics:**
- Implemented as a `msgspec.Struct` (lines 119-122) with `omit_defaults=True` and `dict=True` options, enabling efficient serialization/deserialization
- Uses `@dataclass`-like structure but with msgspec for better performance
- Includes comprehensive docstring (lines 123-203) referencing OpenAI text completion API

**Parameters Related to Your Query (top_p, top_k, min_p):**

```python
top_p: float = 1.0  # Line 212
# Docstring: "Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens 
# to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens."

top_k: int = 0  # Line 213
# Docstring: "Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
# to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens."

min_p: float = 0.0  # Line 214
# Docstring: "Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be 
# considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token. 
# Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this."
```

**Other Notable Sampling Parameters:**
- `n`: Number of output sequences (default: 1)
- `temperature`: Randomness control (default: 1.0) - critical for sampling strategy
- `presence_penalty`, `frequency_penalty`, `repetition_penalty`: Token penalty controls
- `seed`: For reproducible random generation
- `stop`, `stop_token_ids`: Generation termination
- `logprobs`, `prompt_logprobs`: Log probability tracking
- `bad_words`: Token exclusion list
- `guided_decoding`: Structured output control (with GuidedDecodingParams)
- `logit_bias`: Token-specific bias
- `allowed_token_ids`: Token allowlist

### 2. Validation Logic

**Validation Method:** `_verify_args()` (lines 387-462)

**Specific Validation for Key Parameters:**

**top_p validation (line 417-418):**
```python
if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
```
- Must be strictly greater than 0 and less than or equal to 1
- Allows inclusive endpoint at 1.0

**top_k validation (lines 420-425):**
```python
# quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
if self.top_k < -1:
    raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                     f"got {self.top_k}.")
if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
    raise TypeError(
        f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
```
- Must be integer type
- Can be -1 (disabled) or >= 1, or 0 (disabled)
- Rejects values < -1

**min_p validation (lines 426-428):**
```python
if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
                     f"{self.min_p}.")
```
- Must be in closed interval [0, 1] (inclusive on both ends)
- Allows 0.0 to disable

**Temperature-Based Logic (lines 377-382):**
When temperature < epsilon (1e-5), greedy sampling is triggered:
```python
if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
    # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
    self.top_p = 1.0
    self.top_k = 0
    self.min_p = 0.0
    self._verify_greedy_sampling()
```
- Automatically overrides top_p, top_k, and min_p for deterministic (greedy) sampling
- For greedy sampling, n must equal 1 (line 465-467)

**Other Critical Validations:**
- Temperature must be non-negative (line 414-416)
- presence_penalty, frequency_penalty must be in [-2.0, 2.0] (lines 404-409)
- repetition_penalty must be > 0 (lines 410-413)
- max_tokens, min_tokens relationships validated (lines 429-438)
- best_of >= n requirement (lines 335-338, 393-403)

### 3. Post-Initialization Processing

**`__post_init__()` Method (lines 327-385):**

The post-initialization handles:
1. **best_of/n normalization**: If best_of is set, it becomes n and original n becomes _real_n
2. **Temperature clamping**: Warns if temperature is too low (< 1e-2) and clamps it
3. **Seed normalization**: Converts -1 seed to None
4. **Stop handling**: Converts stop string to list, ensures non-empty strings
5. **Bad words handling**: Initializes empty list if None
6. **Logprobs conversion**: Converts boolean True to integer 1
7. **Stop token ID collection**: Updates _all_stop_token_ids set
8. **Output buffer length**: Calculates for stop string evaluation

### 4. Serialization and Deserialization

**Mechanism: msgspec.Struct with JSON Support**

The class uses msgspec's efficient binary serialization:
- `msgspec.Struct` provides automatic msgpack serialization
- Supports JSON encoding/decoding through msgspec.json
- `dict=True` parameter enables dictionary-like access
- Custom fields like `logits_processors` use `Any` type due to msgspec limitations (line 231)

**Key Serialization Patterns:**
- Fields are marked with `msgspec.field()` for special handling (e.g., default factories)
- Constraints encoded via `msgspec.Meta()` for validation hints:
  ```python
  truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
  ```

### 5. Conversion from API Requests to SamplingParams

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331278241-tecb6b/vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

**Factory Method: `SamplingParams.from_optional()` (lines 252-325)**

This static factory method:
- Normalizes None values to defaults
- Clamps logit_bias between -100 and 100 per OpenAI spec
- Converts token IDs in logit_bias to integers
- Returns a new SamplingParams instance

**Request Conversion Methods:**

**ChatCompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()** (lines 452-543):
- Applies request-level defaults
- Falls back to model/engine defaults
- Handles response_format for guided decoding
- Processes tool choices
- Sets output_kind based on streaming

**CompletionRequest.to_sampling_params()** (lines 897-981):
- Similar logic with different defaults
- Handles echo_without_generation case

**Default Parameters Template:**
```python
_DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS: dict = {
    "repetition_penalty": 1.0,
    "temperature": 1.0,
    "top_p": 1.0,
    "top_k": 0,
    "min_p": 0.0,
}
```

### 6. Helper Methods and Properties

**`update_from_generation_config()`** (lines 469-493):
- Updates stop_token_ids from model's generation config
- Adds EOS token IDs appropriately

**`update_from_tokenizer()`** (lines 495-529):
- Tokenizes bad_words into token IDs
- Handles prefix space variations
- Validates token IDs against vocabulary

**`sampling_type` property** (lines 531-537):
- Returns SamplingType enum (GREEDY, RANDOM, or RANDOM_SEED)
- Based on temperature and seed values

**`clone()` method** (lines 548-561):
- Deep copy with special handling for LogitsProcessor objects
- Handles parallel decoding scenarios

### 7. Related Classes

**GuidedDecodingParams** (lines 32-107):
- Separate dataclass for structured output constraints
- Mutually exclusive fields: json, regex, choice, grammar, json_object
- Supports multiple backends with validation

**BeamSearchParams** (lines 591-602):
- Separate parameters for beam search decoding
- Similar but distinct from standard sampling

**RequestOutputKind enum** (lines 109-116):
- Controls output format: CUMULATIVE, DELTA, FINAL_ONLY

### 8. Testing and Examples

**Test Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331278241-tecb6b/tests/test_sampling_params.py`: Unit tests for parameter validation
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331278241-tecb6b/tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py`: End-to-end integration tests

**Common Parameter Combinations Tested:**
- Basic penalties (temperature, presence_penalty, frequency_penalty, repetition_penalty)
- Probability controls (top_p, top_k, min_p)
- Stop conditions (stop strings, stop_token_ids)
- Output options (detokenize, skip_special_tokens, logprobs)
- Bad words filtering
- Seeded sampling for reproducibility

---

**Key Insights:**
1. **msgspec Usage**: The class leverages msgspec for efficient serialization, crucial for distributed inference scenarios
2. **Validation Strictness**: top_p requires open interval on lower bound (>0) but closed on upper (≤1); min_p uses closed interval [0,1]
3. **Temperature-Driven Logic**: Temperature < 1e-5 completely changes sampling behavior (forces greedy decoding)
4. **Default Fallback Chain**: API request → model config → engine defaults
5. **OpenAI Compatibility**: Closely follows OpenAI's text completion API specification while adding vLLM-specific extensions

148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make

class SamplingParams(
        msgspec.Struct,
        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
        # required for @cached_property.
        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
    """Sampling parameters for text generation.

    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.

    Args:
        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
            tokens.
        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
            repeat tokens.
        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
            the model to repeat tokens.
        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
            the stop tokens are special tokens.
        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
            can complete the sequence.
        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
            output text. Defaults to False.
        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
            a first argument.
        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
            Defaults to None.
        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
            sampling implementations.
    """

    n: int = 1
    best_of: Optional[int] = None
    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = 0
    min_p: float = 0.0
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
    min_tokens: int = 0
    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
    detokenize: bool = True
    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE

    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
    # They are set in post_init.
    output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)

    # Fields used to construct logits processors
    guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None

    # Fields used for bad words

class SamplingTensors:
    """Tensors for sampling."""

    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor

    @classmethod
    def from_sampling_metadata(
        cls,
        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
        output_tokens: list[array] = []
        top_ks: list[int] = []
        temperatures: list[float] = []
        top_ps: list[float] = []
        min_ps: list[float] = []
        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
        do_penalties = False
        do_top_p_top_k = False
        do_min_p = False

        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
            min_p = sampling_params.min_p

            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
                temperature = 1.0
            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
                do_top_p_top_k = True
            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
                do_min_p = True
            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
                do_penalties = True

            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
                # their logprobs
                query_len = seq_group.query_len
                assert query_len is not None
                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len

            if seq_group.do_sample:
                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens

        if do_penalties:
            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
                if (seq_group.is_prompt
                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                    prompt_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                    output_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                if seq_group.do_sample:
                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)

        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
            temperatures,
            top_ps,
            top_ks,
            min_ps,
            presence_penalties,
            frequency_penalties,
            repetition_penalties,
            prompt_tokens,
            output_tokens,
            vocab_size,
            device,
            dtype,
        )
        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)

    @classmethod
    def from_lists(
        cls,
        temperatures: list[float],
        top_ps: list[float],
        top_ks: list[int],

@classmethod
    def from_lists(
        cls,
        temperatures: list[float],
        top_ps: list[float],
        top_ks: list[int],
        min_ps: list[float],
        presence_penalties: list[float],
        frequency_penalties: list[float],
        repetition_penalties: list[float],
        prompt_tokens: list[array],
        output_tokens: list[array],
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> "SamplingTensors":
        # Note that the performance will be very bad without
        # pinned memory.

            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)

        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
        do_min_p = self._do_min_p

        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)

        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
        if do_penalties:
            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)

        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))

        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)

        if do_min_p:
            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)

        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
        # Compute the probabilities.
        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
        # Compute the log probabilities.
        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)

        # Sample the next tokens.
        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
            probs,
            logprobs,
            sampling_metadata,
            sampling_tensors,
            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
        )

        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
        else:
            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
            # GPU-side tensors.
            on_device_tensors = None

        # Get the logprobs query results.
        prompt_logprobs = None
        sample_logprobs = None
        if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
            # Pythonize logprobs now (GPU -> CPU); do not defer.
            assert not isinstance(maybe_deferred_sample_results,
                                  SampleResultArgsType)
            prompt_logprobs, sample_logprobs = get_logprobs(
                logprobs, sampling_metadata, maybe_deferred_sample_results)

        return _build_sampler_output(
            maybe_deferred_sample_results,
            sampling_metadata,
            prompt_logprobs,
            sample_logprobs,
            on_device_tensors=on_device_tensors,
            skip_sampler_cpu_output=sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output)

def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
        have not been generated yet
    """
    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
    logits_applied = 0
    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params

        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
        if not seq_group.do_sample:
            continue

        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)

            if seqs_to_penalize:
                # convert to the index into logits
                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
                logits_to_penalize.extend(
                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))

    if logits_to_penalize:
        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")

    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
    return logits


def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Apply top-k.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get all the top_k values.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Apply top-p.
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    # at least one
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits


def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))

    return logits


def _greedy_sample(
    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
    samples: torch.Tensor,
) -> SampleResultType:
    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.

    Args:
        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
            seq_group.do_sample is False.
    Returns:
        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
    """

225	class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
226	
227	    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
228	                 device: DeviceLikeType):
229	        super().__init__()
230	        self.min_p_count: int = 0
231	
232	        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
233	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
234	                                            device="cpu",
235	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
236	        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
237	        # Pre-allocated device tensor
238	        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
239	                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
240	                                                      device=device)
241	        # Current slice of the device tensor
242	        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
243	
244	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
245	        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
246	        return True
247	
248	    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
249	        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])
250	
251	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
252	        if not batch_update:
253	            return
254	
255	        needs_update = False
256	        # Process added requests.
257	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
258	            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
259	            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
260	                needs_update = True
261	                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
262	            if min_p:
263	                self.min_p_count += 1
264	
265	        if self.min_p_count:
266	            # Process removed requests.
267	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
268	            for index in batch_update.removed:
269	                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
270	                    self.min_p_count -= 1
271	
272	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
273	            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
274	                change = (min_p_a :=
275	                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
276	                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
277	                needs_update |= change
278	                if change:
279	                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
280	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
281	                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b
282	
283	        # Update tensors if needed.
284	        size = batch_update.batch_size
285	        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
286	            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
287	            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
288	            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)
289	
290	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
291	        if not self.min_p_count:
292	            return logits
293	
294	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
295	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
296	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
297	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
298	                                       dim=-1,
299	                                       keepdim=True)
300	        # Adjust min_p
301	        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
302	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
303	        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
304	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
305	        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
306	        return logits

@dataclass
class LogitsProcessorManager:
    """Encapsulates initialized logitsproc objects."""
    argmax_invariant: list[LogitsProcessor] = field(
        default_factory=list)  # argmax-invariant logitsprocs
    non_argmax_invariant: list[LogitsProcessor] = field(
        default_factory=list)  # non-argmax-invariant logitsprocs

    @property
    def all(self) -> Iterator[LogitsProcessor]:
        """Iterator over all logits processors."""
        return chain(self.argmax_invariant, self.non_argmax_invariant)


###### ----- Built-in LogitsProcessor impls below here


class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
                 device: DeviceLikeType):
        super().__init__()
        self.min_p_count: int = 0

        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
                                            dtype=torch.float32,
                                            device="cpu",
                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        # Pre-allocated device tensor
        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
                                                      device=device)
        # Current slice of the device tensor
        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]

    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
        return True

    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])

    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
        if not batch_update:
            return

        needs_update = False
        # Process added requests.
        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
                needs_update = True
                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
            if min_p:
                self.min_p_count += 1

        if self.min_p_count:
            # Process removed requests.
            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
            for index in batch_update.removed:
                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
                    self.min_p_count -= 1

            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
                change = (min_p_a :=
                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
                needs_update |= change
                if change:
                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b

        # Update tensors if needed.
        size = batch_update.batch_size
        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.min_p_count:
            return logits

        # Convert logits to probability distribution
        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
                                       dim=-1,
                                       keepdim=True)
        # Adjust min_p
        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits


class LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, pin_memory: bool, device: torch.device):
        super().__init__()
        self.biases: dict[int, dict[int, float]] = {}
        self.device = device
        self.pin_memory = pin_memory

        self.bias_tensor: torch.Tensor = torch.tensor(())
        self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor([], torch.int32),
                             self._device_tensor([], torch.int32))

    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        """Logit bias can rebalance token probabilities and change the
        outcome of argmax in greedy sampling."""
        return False

    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
        if not batch_update:
            return
